physicalDetails.detail: Young human mage, about twenty, with ink-stained fingers, sleepless eyes, and a desk half-buried under copied notices and route sheets.
Reads physically like a man holding back a flood with paper: overworked, under-slept, and steadier the moment he can put his hands on the evidence.
Chapter 8 dresses Corin in borrowed clerk-normalcy — head down, ledger under one arm, trying to look like one more tired city functionary even while his nerves are close to failure.

voiceMarkers.detail: Opens in anxious verbal spill, tripping over ranks and titles until Jaina snaps him back into breathing and task order.
Once he is talking through pages and ward flares, his voice settles into fast, practical explanation with no self-pity.
He is less verbally spilling than in chapter 7; chapter 8 gives him fearful compression, the sort of speech that breaks only when the city proves he had not been paranoid enough.

beliefs.belief: The grain irregularities, sickness clusters, and ward flares are one problem even if the bureaucracy keeps filing them as separate failures.
Copies and hidden samples matter because the official record can be made to vanish.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 7 by the missing reports and moved residue sets.

beliefs.belief: The network inside Andorhal is deeper than any clerk or junior mage can survive proving alone.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 8 when the city answers the capture with immediate search geometry and remote witness silencing.

beliefs.belief: Records only matter if they are split, copied, and hidden; any single cache is already a future loss.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 8 when Jaina and Havel agree to divide the evidence after the breakthrough.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the city doors quiet and support the same-night capture with access, records, and mage-side discretion.
goalsHistory.underlying: Make the weeks of ignored warning become something actionable before the same machinery can erase the proof again.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Keep Andorhal usable by splitting records, preserving caches, and helping Havel seed false trails after the core field team leaves.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Make sure the breakthrough survives contact with the city long enough to support the next sweep north and east.

emotionalStateHistory.state: Relieved, frightened, and newly steadied by being believed at last.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Frightened, vindicated, and sobered by the scale of the response; belief has turned into heavier work rather than relief.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Copied reports before they could disappear upward and hid residue samples after earlier sets went missing from the archive.
Gets the party through exchange offices, shelves, and a leak-prone assay storeroom without advertising the investigation to the whole quarter.
Takes Jaina to the residue room and repack floor instead of freezing at the weight of rank or danger.
Keeps walking through the lane like a late-night clerk during the capture so the snatch has one less visible rupture.
Adds the local rumor and academic memory that let Kel'Thuzad's name become speakable instead of remaining only a broken clue.
Accepts the stay-behind assignment and the split-cache order, choosing city work over the glamour of riding with the main hunt.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Reads ward-flare pattern and route-book irregularity well enough to recognize a city-scale magical and logistical conspiracy before anyone local will honor it.
Preserves evidence under institutional pressure by copying, hiding, and reordering records faster than they can vanish.
Navigates Kirin Tor and civic bureaucracy tactically, knowing which door, shelf, or functionary can still be used under pressure.
Can hold mundane cover under active operation despite obvious fear, which is exactly what the snatch requires from him.
Turns rumor, copied paperwork, and institutional memory into a credible identity fragment when no surviving witness can finish the confession cleanly.
Understands evidence survival at the cache level, not just the page level, making him more useful after the breakthrough than during the immediate chase.

howOthersAddressHim.speaker: Jaina Proudmoore
howOthersAddressHim.pattern: Uses his name immediately and strips the panic out of the room by turning him from nervous subordinate into working contact.

howOthersAddressHim.speaker: Sylvanas Windrunner
howOthersAddressHim.pattern: Treats him with clipped field directness once he proves he has the right pages.

howHeAddressesOthers.pattern: Begins with over-deferential rank confusion, then stabilizes once she gives him permission to be useful.
Measures his speech carefully around them at first, then speaks plainly once he realizes they care more about proof than ceremony.
Forgets himself into calling him master through Jaina's stone connection, revealing how deeply Antonidas still structures his sense of authority.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Jolts upright too fast when Jaina names herself, then becomes visibly steadier with paper under his hands.
Clears desk space by moving notice towers to the floor and lays sheets out in the order his mind prefers, turning anxiety into procedure.
Keeps his head down and his pace ordinary in the lane even while the operation turns around him.
Goes pale enough for the others to read it when the city proves the response was prepared all along.

knowledge.detail: Knows six weeks of route marks east out of Andorhal do not match the official shortage story.
Knows the granary wards flare on nights when no licensed spellwork should be happening.
Knows reports to Dalaran and the local magistracy have been absorbed or dismissed rather than acted on.
Knows where the residue samples and copied records can still be worked without clerks breathing through the door.
Learns the city can close a district around a missed report window almost immediately.
Connects the north-of-city scholar rumors to Kel'Thuzad once the other fragments align.
Knows Andorhal is only the throat of the system and that the grain has already moved into wider Lordaeron routes.
Witnesses Antonidas hearing the name, which confirms the field discovery has crossed into Kirin Tor awareness.
Understands by chapter end that his role is local continuity: split caches, split records, keep the city costly for the enemy to clean.

arcHistory.currentPosition: Chapter 7 introduces Corin as an ignored junior mage who becomes trusted internal access for the Andorhal breach.

arc.lie: If he cannot make the bureaucracy act, then his evidence may not matter at all.
arc.truth: The pattern was real, and once the right people arrive his buried work becomes load-bearing.
arc.currentPosition: Chapter 8 moves Corin from ignored junior witness to indispensable local steward of the evidence. He is still frightened, but now he is frightened in the mode of someone trusted with the aftermath, not dismissed as a nervous apprentice.
